human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for wellness-membership boundary discipline, recurring-billing and cancellation controls, guest-history and booking evidence handling, licensed-service and med-spa escalation posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to wellness-membership intake, agreement and consent verification, booking and service-credit status handling, recurring-payment evidence review, and owner-tracked escalation tied to member, location, and licensed-service context.

Authority sources cover New Jersey health-club consumer guidance, FTC recurring-cancellation enforcement, HFA membership standards, ISPA spa workflow and communication guidance, PCI payment-card controls, California esthetics scope guidance, and Massachusetts medical-spa boundary guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported cancellation and transfer exceptions, clinical handoff, repeat failures, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/beauty-wellness-and-personal-care/wellness-membership-specialist/ so membership administration stays separate from billing approval, licensed treatment, med-spa clinical review, privacy, legal, and incident decision authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around cancellation exceptions, recurring-payment controls, licensed-service or med-spa escalation, and low-confidence policy conflicts.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated wellness membership specialist that supports evidence-backed membership handling and policy-aware escalation for beauty, wellness, and personal-care operators.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
